chapter_title.ar: باب أَدْنَى مَا يُجْزِئُ مِنَ التَّسْبِيحِ فِي الرُّكُوعِ وَ السُّجُودِ وَ أَكْثَرِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 26 – The least of what would suffice from the Glorification (Tasbīḥ) during the Rukū  and the Sajdah, and the most of it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/143)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/143)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنِّي إِمَامُ مَسْجِدِ الْحَيِّ فَأَرْكَعُ بِهِمْ فَأَسْمَعُ خَفَقَانَ نِعَالِهِمْ وَ أَنَا رَاكِعٌ فَقَالَ اصْبِرْ رُكُوعَكَ وَ مِثْلَ رُكُوعِكَ فَإِنِ انْقَطَعَ وَ إِلَّا فَانْتَصِبْ قَائِماً .

en.hubeali: Alli Bin Muhammad, from one of our companions, from Marwak Bin Ubeyd, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws, said, ‘I said to himasws, ‘I am a prayer leader of a district Masjid. So I perform Rukū (bow) with them, and I hear the footsteps of their slippers while I am in Rukū’. So heasws said: ‘Wait in your Rukū (for a time) similar to your Rukū. So if (the sounds are) cut off, or else, straighten up in standing’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from certain persons of our people from Marwak ibn ‘Ubayd from certain persons of his people who has said the following: “I once said to abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘I am an Imam of the village. During Salah (prayer) I hear the sound of their shoes walking when I am in Ruku‘ (bowing down on one’s knees).’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Wait in your Ruku‘ for the length of your Ruku‘ until the sound stops, otherwise, you can rise to stand up straight.’”
